citation.anchor: Returns the number of instruction and data debug register pairs

preconditions: The PAL static firmware gate is available
The product implements sixteen IBRs and sixteen DBRs with no attached hardware debugger
inputs: Each of the three reserved arguments independently set to one
expected_results: The valid call returns status zero, eight instruction pairs, eight data pairs, and a zero reserved result
Each invalid call returns -2 with three zero payload results
allowed_results: No debugger-reserved pair reduction is permitted because this synthetic product exposes no attached hardware debugger
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: Each call publishes exactly one complete status and payload tuple
cases.restart_action: Each call returns directly to its declared continuation without retry

oracle.derivation: The procedure index, argument rules, status, pair-count units, and return layout are test-owned literals from the cited PAL contract; the eight-pair product count is independently matched to the complete sixteen-register selector test.

limitations: Hardware-debugger pair reservation is not implemented or advertised by this synthetic platform

citation.anchor: Apart from the PMC and PMD register files, access of a non-existent register results in a Reserved Register/Field fault

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled and serialized
Every selector and value GR is NaT clear
The General Exception handler validates ISR.code 0x30 and resumes after the faulting bundle
inputs: Distinct valid images for DBR0 through DBR15
Both access directions for every selector 16 through 255
expected_results: All sixteen implemented images survive delayed readback without aliasing
Every reserved selector faults once in each direction
Fault count is 480 and both mismatch counters are zero
allowed_results: The diagnostic exception record may retain the final delivered General Exception after RFI completion
permitted_state_changes: Read destinations and handler observation registers
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
expected_faults: Reserved Register/Field for every read and write of DBR16 through DBR255
cases.committed_prefix: Implemented writes commit before delayed readback; reserved accesses commit only handler counters
cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, advance CR.IIP by one bundle, and execute RFI for each of 480 faults

result_observations: terminal IP and final exception record

oracle.derivation: The sixteen-register boundary, low-eight-bit selector rule, reserved-fault code, 240-by-two fault count, and pairwise-distinct values are test-owned constants derived from the cited instruction contract and declared product profile.

limitations: Debug-match address behavior is covered by the existing architectural debug test and is not re-claimed as selector-space evidence

citation.anchor: For all register files other than the region registers, bits {7:0} of GR r3 are used as the index

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled and serialized
Every selector and value GR is NaT clear
The General Exception handler validates ISR.code 0x30 and resumes after the faulting bundle
inputs: Distinct valid images for IBR0 through IBR15

expected_results: All sixteen implemented images survive delayed readback without aliasing

cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, advance CR.IIP by one bundle, and execute RFI for each of 480 faults

citation.anchor: Even numbered registers contain breakpoint addresses, odd registers contain breakpoint mask conditions

preconditions: A data serialization boundary follows the complete write sequence
inputs: Eight pairwise-distinct addresses and eight pairwise-distinct legal control images
expected_results: Even DBRs preserve all 64 address bits
Odd DBRs preserve mask55:0, PLM59:56, w62, and r63
All sixteen register images remain distinct
allowed_results: No debug fault is required because PSR.db remains clear during field traversal
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: Every DBR write becomes visible after the required data serialization boundary
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for implemented CPL0 accesses

oracle.derivation: The even/odd layout, exact field bit ranges, ignored-bit mask, and serialization kind are encoded as test-owned literals from the cited register tables.

limitations: Every debug-match access class and IA-32 behavior remain separate closure units

citation.anchor: All 64 bits are implemented on all processors regardless of the number of implemented address bits

preconditions: An instruction serialization boundary follows the complete write sequence
inputs: Eight pairwise-distinct addresses and eight pairwise-distinct legal control images
expected_results: Even IBRs preserve all 64 address bits
Odd IBRs preserve mask55:0, PLM59:56, and x63
All sixteen register images remain distinct
allowed_results: No debug fault is required because PSR.db remains clear during field traversal
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: Every IBR write becomes visible after the required instruction serialization boundary
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for implemented CPL0 accesses

oracle.derivation: The even/odd layout, exact field bit ranges, ignored-bit mask, and serialization kind are encoded as test-owned literals from the cited register tables.

limitations: Every instruction-debug matching variant and IA-32 behavior remain separate closure units
